detailText:
internet.password

Generates a random password-like string. Do not use this method for generating actual passwords for users.

Schema: awd.domain.internet.password()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
length	The length of the password to generate. Example: 12
memorable	Whether the generated password should be memorable. Example: true
pattern	The pattern that all chars should match. This option will be ignored, if memorable is true. Example: [A-Z]
prefix	The prefix to use. Example: #
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
length	integer	optional
memorable	boolean	optional
pattern	regexp	optional
prefix	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows internet.password with all optional params omitted.

Command: internet.password

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: internet.password()

Returns: He2AFTHb4tHV3mb

Shows internet.password using only a custom length.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (length=12)

Full call: internet.password(length=12)

Returns: He2AFTHb4tHV

Shows internet.password using only the memorable flag.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (memorable=true)

Full call: internet.password(memorable=true)

Returns: hefutisawetikub

Shows internet.password generating a memorable password-like string.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (length=12, memorable=true)

Full call: internet.password(length=12, memorable=true)

Returns: hefutisaweti

Shows internet.password constrained only by a regex-style pattern.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (pattern="[A-Z]")

Full call: internet.password(pattern="[A-Z]")

Returns: HAFTHHVISKOWXHH

Shows internet.password constrained by a regex-style pattern.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (length=12, memorable=false, pattern="[A-Z]")

Full call: internet.password(length=12, memorable=false, pattern="[A-Z]")

Returns: HAFTHHVISKOW

Shows internet.password using only the prefix option.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (prefix="#")

Full call: internet.password(prefix="#")

Returns: #He2AFTHb4tHV3m

Shows internet.password using length, pattern, and prefix together.

Command: internet.password

Params field: (length=12, memorable=false, pattern="[A-Z]", prefix="#")

Full call: internet.password(length=12, memorable=false, pattern="[A-Z]", prefix="#")

Returns: #HAFTHHVISKO

Open documentation

commerce.price

Generates a price between min and max (inclusive).

Schema: awd.domain.commerce.price()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
dec	The number of decimal places. Example: 2
max	The maximum price. Example: 100
min	The minimum price. Example: 1
symbol	The currency value to use. Example: $
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
dec	integer	optional
max	number	optional
min	number	optional
symbol	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows commerce.price in use.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Full call: commerce.price(dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Returns: $4.79

Shows commerce.price when optional params are omitted.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: commerce.price()

Returns: 417.69

Shows commerce.price using dec.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (dec=2)

Full call: commerce.price(dec=2)

Returns: 417.69

Shows commerce.price using max.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (max=100)

Full call: commerce.price(max=100)

Returns: 42.29

Shows commerce.price using min.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (max=10, min=1)

Full call: commerce.price(max=10, min=1)

Returns: 4.79

Shows commerce.price using symbol.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (symbol="$")

Full call: commerce.price(symbol="$")

Returns: $417.69

Open documentation

helpers.arrayElement

Returns one random element from the supplied array.

Schema: faker.helpers.arrayElement()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
array	Array of candidate values to choose from.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
array	array	required
Usage Examples

Shows helpers.arrayElement in use.

Command: helpers.arrayElement

Params field: (["A", "B", "C"])

Full call: helpers.arrayElement(["A", "B", "C"])

Returns: B

Open documentation

datatype.boolean

Returns the boolean value true or false.

Schema: awd.domain.datatype.boolean()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
probability	Probability threshold for returning true (between 0 and 1).
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
probability	number	optional
Usage Examples

Shows datatype.boolean when optional params are omitted.

Command: datatype.boolean

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: datatype.boolean()

Returns: true

Shows datatype.boolean using probability.

Command: datatype.boolean

Params field: (probability=0.5)

Full call: datatype.boolean(probability=0.5)

Returns: true

Open documentation

internet.httpMethod

Returns a random HTTP request method from an AnywayData-defined pool of GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS, TRACE, and CONNECT, with optional filtering for common methods and exclusions.

Schema: awd.domain.internet.httpMethod()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
commonOnly	When true, limits generation to GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, and DELETE. Defaults to false. Example: true
excludes	Comma-separated HTTP methods to remove from the candidate set. Values are case-insensitive, surrounding spaces are trimmed, and generation throws if exclusions remove every available method. Example: patch, TRACE
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
commonOnly	boolean	optional
excludes	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows internet.httpMethod choosing from the full HTTP method set by default.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: internet.httpMethod()

Returns: PUT

Shows internet.httpMethod restricted to the common request methods.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: (commonOnly=true)

Full call: internet.httpMethod(commonOnly=true)

Returns: POST

Shows internet.httpMethod trimming spaces, normalizing case, and excluding methods from the full set.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: (excludes="patch, TRACE")

Full call: internet.httpMethod(excludes="patch, TRACE")

Returns: POST

Open documentation
